dependencies: Single source a number of parts and raw materials critical to business operations

Delays may be caused by factors affecting suppliers including raw material availability, capacity constraints, port congestion, labor disputes, shortages of labor, economic downturns, availability of credit, impaired financial condition, sanctions/tariffs, energy inflation/availability, suppliers' allocations to other purchasers, weather emergencies, natural disasters, acts of government or acts of war or terrorism
risks: Vulnerability to raw material, transportation and labor price fluctuations and supply shortages
Supply chain disruptions and related challenges throughout the supply chain
Material, transportation, labor and other cost inflation
Disruption risk in certain categories of supply chains
Climate change effects including extreme weather events may exacerbate supply chain risks

regulatory_risks: Settlement Agreements with EPA, CARB, DOJ and California Attorney General's Office regarding emissions certification and compliance process for certain engines
Failure to comply with Settlement Agreements subjects to stipulated penalties

Products subject to extensive statutory and regulatory requirements governing emissions and noise including standards imposed by EPA, EU, state regulatory agencies (CARB) and other regulatory agencies around the world
Increased scrutiny from regulatory agencies
Unpredictability in adoption, implementation and enforcement of increasingly stringent and fragmented emission standards by multiple jurisdictions around the world
Evolving environmental and climate change legislation and regulatory initiatives
New legal or regulatory requirements including those designed to reduce or mitigate carbon content or effects of GHG emissions
EPA proposal to designate two widely used PFAS as hazardous substances
EPA published final rule imposing reporting and recordkeeping requirements on manufacturers and importers of PFAS
Changes in international, national and regional trade laws, regulations and policies affecting and/or restricting international trade

Embargoes, sanctions and export controls imposed by U.S. and other governments restricting or prohibiting transactions with certain persons or entities, to certain countries or regions, or involving certain products
Changing U.S. export controls and sanctions on China and other restrictions affecting transactions involving China and Chinese parties
Complex system of commercial and trade regulations around the world
Foreign regulatory frameworks more stringent or complex than similar regimes in United States
Development and enforcement of laws regarding trade compliance and anti-corruption such as U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act
New regulatory requirements regarding data privacy such as European Union General Data Protection Regulation
Expected global sustainability regulations
California government officials called for state to phase out sales of certain diesel-powered vehicles by 2035
Future bans or limitations on use of diesel-powered vehicles or other applications
Multiple countries and cities announced plans to implement ban on use of diesel-powered products

sanction_risks: Embargoes, sanctions and export controls could limit sales of products

Changing U.S. export controls and sanctions on China could affect ability to collect receivables, access cash generated in China, provide aftermarket and warranty support, sell products and impact reputation and business
Indefinite suspension of Russian operations due to ongoing conflict in Ukraine
Impairment of joint venture with KAMAZ in Russia
